summary: Frozen HEAD e64469202935397c70b215d3ef069f10809128a1 passes release-risk review. Exact-head unit 288/288, integration 4/4, E2E 4/4 with 8/8 AC coverage, typecheck, and build-equivalent validation pass; failure and recovery paths remain typed and fail closed; target conformance remains improved from 81 to 80 because the post-conformance delta changes no architecture or production source. The autonomous boundary still ends at current-HEAD final_prepare and reuses the shipped runtime-connector and independent-review owners, while PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, and material external effects remain explicit human operations. This verdict is not merge authorization: PR #385 currently exposes pre-closure HEAD 926227f945878299770448a03966c17dfa70158d, so e6446920 must be pushed and its current-head CI imported before merge.
inspection_summary: Inspected the formal strict-HEAD request, clean frozen Git state and full origin/main surface, the production run-session owner and public CLI boundary, managed-worktree HEAD enforcement, bounded needs_changes repair and fresh re-review, typed auth/quota/permission/timeout/cancel/provider/malformed/no-progress/CI/Gate stops, legacy and disable-autonomous rollback paths, exact-head unit/integration/E2E/typecheck/build records, conformance provenance, final Story/roadmap/registry closure commits, predecessor ownership, and live GitHub PR #385 head/check/merge surface. The current local release candidate is internally consistent; remote PR/CI rebinding remains a required later delivery step.
inspection_inputs: git rev-parse HEAD; git status --short; git diff --check origin/main...HEAD; git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the final completed-state commits could silently broaden release authority or alter runtime behavior -> Final judgment: the delta after 926227f9 is limited to Story/roadmap/registry closure, behavioral E2E closure assertions, and bounded review accounting; production source and the seven-action authority boundary are unchanged.

Initial concern: failure coverage might prove only the happy path -> Final judgment: exact-head tests cover malformed runtime results and decisions, auth denial, quota, permission waits, provider unavailability/fallback, start/status/result timeouts, cancellation containment and stale completion, no-progress, verification failure, needs_changes repair/re-review, stale Gate, and CI pending with typed non-green outcomes and persisted recovery.

Initial concern: the latest conformance record is bound to 926227f9 rather than e6446920 -> Final judgment: the 81-to-80 result remains applicable to the inspected content surface because every later commit changes only config, Story/roadmap text, and E2E assertions; no target-model or production dependency edge changed, and the exact-head architecture review independently confirms no run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency.

Initial concern: completed Story state could duplicate predecessor implementations -> Final judgment: guarded-run-session composes agent-runtime-connectors and independent-review-orchestrator as existing owners; the closure owner remains a thin seven-action composition and the post-closure E2E explicitly preserves PR #377 and PR #382 lineage.

Initial concern: a clean local candidate might be mistaken for a current PR release surface -> Final judgment: live GitHub inspection shows PR #385 is open, non-draft, mergeable/CLEAN, and green on Node 20/22 plus CodeQL, but still points to pre-closure HEAD 926227f9. Therefore the code-risk verdict passes while push, current-head CI, CI import, canonical Gate rerun, and explicit execute merge remain mandatory and are not waived by this review.
